meta.note: Hand-curated cross-tool composition idioms — goal -> pipeline. tldr-pages teaches what ONE tool does; this file teaches how tools COMPOSE for a goal. Add entries here as you discover patterns worth surfacing. Each entry's `tools` list is the set of binaries that must be present for the idiom to be injected (so we don't suggest mlr to hosts without it). Edit by hand; nothing rebuilds this file.

composition.intent: count unique values of a column across many lines (e.g. caller IPs, status codes)
composition.cmd: rg -o 'PATTERN' file | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head

composition.intent: tally HTTP status codes in an access log (col 9 of nginx default format)
composition.cmd: mlr --inidx --ifs ' ' cut -f 9 access.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

composition.intent: find files modified in last 24h and grep them in one shot
show the 20 largest files in a tree
show directory tree by size, depth-limited
pretty-print every JSON file in a tree
extract one field across many JSON-lines files
convert a JSON array of objects to CSV
pretty-print a CSV in the terminal
compute mean/min/max of a CSV column
interactively pick a file and open it in $EDITOR
interactively pick a process and kill it
interactively pick a git branch and check it out
composition.cmd: git branch --format='%(refname:short)' | fzf | xargs -r git checkout

composition.intent: re-run tests on every source-file change
benchmark two command variants, with warmup + stats
find all processes matching a pattern (atomic, no grep|grep dance)
kill all processes matching a pattern
run N copies of a command in parallel over a list of args
composition.cmd: parallel -j N CMD ::: arg1 arg2 arg3

composition.intent: process every line of stdin in parallel
show side-by-side colored diff (or use as git diff pager)
composition.cmd: delta a.txt b.txt    # or: git config core.pager delta

composition.intent: tail a log with live colored highlighting of matches
write to a file you're also reading (avoid clobbering with truncation)
monitor bytes streaming through a pipe
extract a line range from a file (read-only-what-you-need)
composition.cmd: sed -n '100,150p' file    # or: awk 'NR>=100 && NR<=150' file

composition.intent: find then act in-place across files (safer than -exec ; or | xargs)
watch a command repeatedly with diff highlighting
show free disk in human form across all mounts
hex-dump a file with color, paged
syntax-highlighted view in terminal (NOT for piping into other tools)
composition.cmd: bat file.go    # use plain `cat` when piping

composition.intent: simple in-place substitution across files
